It is not the least bit exciting to me that we have recently come to the rescue of New Zealand radio broadcaster Dave McTigle, who has been spending the last two weeks living for himself in the pirate paradise of Gotze Island. Now he has released an update on the radio channel, which means three more tunes will be on air on Monday, he announced earlier this week.


The first will be 'The Starving Man' by Skepta, followed by 'The Turning Moon' by Gpm. All four tunes are available as a download, and any of the other changes will be available via the download button below, according to the official release page. The other tunes are 'The Ship's Odd Lead Theory', 'Last of the Sea's Shadow', and 'The Eternals Ahead'.


The last tune, 'The Thing Called the Earth', was originally planned for 'The Sunshine That Never Was' but that was cancelled due to UK copyright concerns. McTigle's new tune was only available in the US, but the UK also has a licence to swap tracks of his song with the same track. Thankfully, he's still available on vinyl as well.


Here's how Dave's announcement came out:


Alan Williams - Sound-Editor - Radio New Zealand

Dave's first radio job was working on the BBC's Radio New Zealand (now defunct) in the mid-1990s, as part of the former UK-based Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group. He reported late at night on Sky High, and occasionally played at BBC Radio New Zealand's Cape Town headquarters, as well as at the station's radio station in Gold St. George.


He joined the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group in 1987, when he was in the middle of developing a new audio branding tool called the World Warsheets, and then proceeded to become Wall Street's most successful producer. On the radio front, he ran the Secretariat of Radio New Zealand, and also ran the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group's partner station Station Fire! Shuttles.


He is survived by his wife Sarah Barnes and two sons Terry and Mark (the Dead Kennedys) and nine grandchildren. The organisation does not take kindly to such public criticism since in a May 8th blog post, drummer Richard Leeson wrote: ", Dave's assignment was to produce [the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group's] own radio stations, in addition to the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group's remaining radio broadcasters.


"This meant that in the first three months of 1988, the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group had its first live broadcast with Dave's members. The station and its company, the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group, broadcast four years and eight months of audio content. Dave and his staff received appropriate royalties to cover the cost of the commentary, project management and so on."


Dave, along with the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group and its affiliate station, Radio New Zealand, has been in business since 1987 with a focus on growing the radio industry through two public radio stations. In 1988, Dave's last televised undertaking was in New Zealand, when he merged with a new company called Radio New Zealand.


You can download Dave McTigle's first two tunes as a CD download here. And you can watch Dave's live show on Radio New Zealand today - click on the button above.


Dave McTigle released new track 'The Samurai' last month via the Radio New Zealand Broadcasting Group's music channel, Adrift.

In the preliminary phase of international transparency, the United States has been especially concerned about the quality of telecommunications nationwide, a fact that has led the Federal Communications Commission to renege on its previous agreements. The FCC is testing and evaluating the effectiveness of partnership agreements with ISPs, and there is also concern about an increase in the number of Title II broadband Internet service providers that receive the same efficiencies from the bulk of telecommunications customers.


The new letter provides guidance from the FCC on the appropriate process to address that concern, including whether to rely on a fully capable partnership and its processes.


The letter provides technical guidance to guide the FCC's efforts to ensure that no untruths are translated into new policies that hamper consumer choice.


The letter further states that the FCC needs to provide greater clarity about a significant portion of the "content pages" in its communications infrastructure. These include those provided by providers, their customers, and government entities, such as the Department of Commerce, and the public service as part of the Internet's backbone. The FCC's reliance on the fact that the vast majority of broadband services in the United States are sold by a single ISP is unnecessary in a time when the average home phone bill in the United States is around $16,000. The FCC's reliance on the fact that broadband services are available on a consistent basis in a single country leaves the Internet vulnerable to repeat attacks on consumers and the government, resulting in the loss of investment and innovation.


The latest letter provides guidance on the required data ratio, which is a key objective for the FCC to achieve. The FCC notes that this requires a more complete look at the policy changes under review for broadband Internet access and that the new letter addresses the the FCC's consideration of data ratio as an example of broadband transparency.


On December 12, 2014, the Commission adopted I-R14-97 which noted that the Commission has "improved" its role as the "commissioner under the authority of the Open Internet Order, the Open Internet Order, the Open Internet Security Act, and the Open Internet Rule of Practice." The Commission adopted I-R14-97 in July, 2015, and I-R14-97 is now available in the public domain, the app store, and the web. The Commission's final legislative report on the Open Internet Order states the following:


In addition to strengthening our statutory authority for the commission, the Commission operates as the "Composite Internet Communications" (CIS), which is a separate form of classification for the Coast Guard. To obtain a CIS, a vessel or vessel class—such as a ship or vessel class—selected for service by the Commission must demonstrate amazing capabilities, as well as be otherwise equipped to perform capture, serial, and content monitoring tasks, such as determining if a given broadband service is in a lawful and competitive position.


COMS: Robert E. M. Noordheim, CIDLP, Communications Director, FCC


IP: 202-797-4168, e-mail: @ip_m@fcc.gov

FROM: FCC Privacy Policy


In the form of the order on consolidation, the FCC plays down how the Internet is being monitored, defending its current national requirements and officials' comfort with the fact that this is the biggest story in the FCC's ongoing digital consolidation effort. But the issue is a bit more nuanced than that. The FCC has always viewed the Internet as an interrelated, standing-room only peewee, privately owned panacea for the nation's problems, and once the Internet is built on private property, the FCC's decisions to ignore its obligations to the U.S. government for privacy and openness are presumably going to have long-lasting consequences.


The ISP that is able to demonstrate excellent privacy, innovative offerings, and promises of high-performance in customer service, and is able to make a good case for the network as the best possible Internet product, the ISP is the one that needs to hear the new rules. Transparency is crucial, but it is relatively easy to attack privacy and openness.


The FCC's privacy issues have been highlighted by the growing number of privacy complaints regarding the congestion that the Internet's traffic is causing. This development explains why the FCC believes the Internet is a time-sensitive, and typically significantly less money-efficient, means for ISPs to better serve their customers. As the Internet gets bigger, the FCC is beginning to ask for more transparency, and the FCC should be encouraging ISPs to send a clear and meaningful message to their customers they're not safe from these sorts of surveillance.

Did you know that the most successful videos on YouTube come from the folks over at the Internet Archive? Check out the four years the Internet Archive has been publishing video on the site.

The four years The Internet Archive has been publishing video on the site are: February 5, 1999- April 21, 2000, 2005- 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, and the as of Dec. 24, 2015, the last day on which the Internet Archive files its annual report on YouTube was Feb. 5, 1999.

So (or rather, because it's Big Appel's birthday, since we have to go to 2012 to get updated statistics), the following video is based on years I had heard of the Internet Archive, including:

UPDATE • There's a note that says something about the YouTube engagement since Dec. 24, 2015.

UPDATE • Some of the media are getting some kind of traffic on YouTube. You can check out the TM Breakdown on YouTube or find out a bit about it here.

The Internet Archive

Now that all is out, let's give some credit where credit is due, because the Internet Archive was born. It started in 1999, and has since seen a great deal of growth. In 2000, the Internet Archive got its start in an effort to save money on maintenance. Its efforts were partly the result of the "Chiliatic Flu" craze, which was an attempt to reduce the connection between the Internet and the public. Today the group is the seventh-largest nonprofit in the world and accounts for more than half of all the revenue from YouTube.

The operation continues to grow, and in the last year, it has supported more than 2,000 charities, mostly independent ones.

The Internet Archive Project

The Internet Archive is copyright-free. As of Dec. 1, 2014, it was the second-largest nonprofit in the world.

The Internet Archive was founded in 1998, and its mission that same year was to "create a better and more comprehensive media, public, legal and community environment for the free and open Internet."

The Internet Archive is also a nonprofit. It funded several major national and international projects, which include the Project Gutenberg, the New York Times Educational, Institutes of Technology, MacArthur Foundation, the Tor Project, the YouTube Foundation, and others.

The Internet Archive's mission has been to create new ways for people to share and not to be harmed in the process. It has pioneered curriculum in many areas. The Archive now publishes a monthly addition to the Web that stays updated daily.

The Internet Archive keeps a database of every YouTube video, which is now more than 300,000 lines of Code, and the web page has more than 4.5 million page views per month. It has also published a year-round archive of over 1,200,000 original videos, including over 1.4 million from 12 countries.

Keeping the Internet Archive up and running

Decubbing the Internet Archive made the Internet Archive the thing to keep up with. It's a community effort, and Forbes has put it as a top ten list of the society's best projects. But if you're a filmmaker and hope to monetize your work, you'll want to stay on top of your filmmaking.

The Internet Archive has been a company for years. It is the client of a company that uses new technology and makes older projects available and, more importantly, better at providing the right tools to help make your videos, videos and guests matter.

So, how did you get involved?

When I was in fifth grade, my mother had a great vial of old school internet service called Patrick's Internet. I bought it when it was offered to me by college students and let them in on how the service worked. And I take a stand. It is my responsibility to make sure it works. In 2006, my parents purchased my school's Internet service for about $5,000.

It is my right to provide an audience. As a kid, I was taught not to listen to commercial music. I impulsively resisted the temptation to listen to electronic music, and more recently, I have listened to many TV shows for the last two years. Although I do not listen to much music at school, I do listen to life music and to a certain extent, I watch movies and television shows.

The Internet Archive has always been a part of my life. It's a protocol that rules me. I don't take responsibility for my own work. I would

The Department of Homeland Security has issued a field report on one of the most dangerous cities in America, San Francisco.

"The Department of Homeland Security has directed the U.S. Department of Homeland Security to respond to the following incidents within the San Francisco Metropolitan area of the United States, including attacks against the commercial transport of firearms and explosives suspected to be directed against civilians or commercial transport of firearms and explosives suspected to be directed against persons of unspecified national origin, including individuals with ties to the U.S.," said the report released Wednesday.

A total of 16 criminal charges were filed against a total of 74 people, including 20 U.S. citizens, according to the summary of the DHS report.

The report lists five potential targets that could be targeted by investigators.

"There have been 24 shootings that occurred in July and August of 2016 between security responder-in-charge and individuals involved in the firearms and explosives. The survey also includes unauthorized individuals aware of the threats," the DHS noted.

"People charged with crimes on a regular basis are being investigated and held accountable. The Government is committed to comprehensive prevention of violent crime by providing resources, support and outreach to local law enforcement to identify and provide options to end violence."

Brighton Town boss Steve McClaren says: "To hear that someone who is an unmatched player and technician is going to miss the game when he is the best player available is absolutely not good.

"This is the biggest match of the season for us, and it is a chance we have to take it into account.

"We were very pleased and we want to keep his fitness as long as possible.

"Nothing has changed in the 20 minutes we played, so we are not going to play against any other team in the Premier League.

"We will look for opportunities to get into the game, that's the way it works, but I think it's been a lot of work for us to get back into the game and show that we can be top of the table."

Clifford County Sheriff's Office Fort Pierce prosecutor Clark Sticheck took step back from his testimony this week after a federal judge expressed his surprise that a five-year sentence from 2007 to 2007 was due.

On Friday, the District Attorney's Office announced it would seek a motion to drop the case, which was argued after a two-month back-and-forth between prosecutors and Sticheck.

"The date of July 2, 2007, in which the defendant was told he was dead and that the grand jury had ruled in his favor, was not the date Buggy said he would turn in his test results," Assistant U.S. Attorney Michael Jones told the press in a statement. "It is my understanding that Mr. McGraw and two other defendants were not notified of the terms of their plea agreement and the appeal indicated that the defendant may subsequently have completed the original sentence of twenty-one years imprisonment under the statutes, the maximum sentence that is required under the United States Constitution."

The two defendants relate their story to the case of the Florida teenager who was sentenced in February 2009 to life in prison without parole for the murder of his sister in a Florida stretch of coastline.

The prosecutor's office said the boy's mother had run over her 5-year-old son on the shore of Sturgeon Lake in Florida in March of 2008 when he was 9. His father said he was frightened and was going to scream and his mother tried to protect him.

The court in St. Pete declined to sentence the man in the death of his sister to life in prison without parole.

Prosecutors said the boy lived with his mother and sister, who was a dental assistant, in murky water off Flagler, Florida. It is possible the boy was underwater in the water and was unconscious when the girl's body was discovered along with the help of their teacher.

Prosecutors said the woman started to fall over when she tried to protect her sister, but her child was not on the water.

"He is not a recovering prisoner who has been financially dependent upon the service of others for the last decade or so," Sticheck said. "He was a troubled individual who did not respond appropriately to the needs of others."

The prosecutor's office said it has taken several steps to ensure the case was dealt with of course. It asked Sticheck, who is starting a new practice in Miami in May, to access the Clem's information on clients who are charged.

"I want to make sure there is no prejudice on this point," Sticheck said in a statement.

But his application for the maximum sentence, which he called an "injustice sentence," fails to work.

"I'm working hard to make sure that what is provided to a person and what is provided to a jury is as fair in that the harshest possible sentence is granted," he said in his statement. "I intend to continue to work to improve litigation and to make sure all of the information is available to the public, including the relevant court for a jury trial, without delay."

Sticheck did not return calls and emails seeking comment Friday.

But the law firm that prosecuted the case forwarded a letter to Sticheck last week, complaining that the state was dealing "with an out of court plea deal." It suggested the state should hire a private attorney for the defendant.

"This is an out of court plea deal," the letter said.

Movie synopsis:

Michael Cera stars in this thrilling thriller in which he, Michael, and his teenage sister go on an exhaustive search for Michael's stolen love interest, the one he called "K.S." (Kris Monster).

Their quest goes awry when Michael suddenly reveals that he was the one who dragged K.S. out of the hospital, and he attends to her every effort to find him. But the tragic end of Michael's life could easily have been avoided. The film is an unspeakable tragedy for audiences that have come to know Michael as a character with a gushy sense of humor and a profound character.

Michael has been missing for four years, and if he's alive, he could end up in prison. Since he was aware of K.S.'s disappearance, he's been plagued by guilt. He blames Michael for ruining his life, but it seems this isn't the only way that he's upended his own character. As Michael's girlfriend, her career choices, and her sexual drive have made her uneasy with him. While taking action, he's also bruised by her ruthless attempts at revenge, so there's a bit of jealousy in this movie. Michael doesn't travel with all the people and events that happen around him, and he doesn't want to also end up in jail. He's too much of a burden to not come forward to confront as much as possible.

As a father, he protects his daughter and he tries to achieve comfort over K.S.'s fate. He has no love for her at all, so he's willing to accept whatever it takes to save her. The future is bleak, but the world is in his hands, and he has to take on K.S. in order to prove his loyalty to her. And he's been doing it for so long that he knows what it is he's going to do and what he's gonna do.

The 3.8-meter (8" male adult, or 1.7-pound) animal recorded on the beaches of the Mombasa village near Soma Bay in the northern Pacific, while its owner on the Bali coast said he spent a week exploring the island's diverse flora and fauna five months ago to see what was out there.

The crocodile's unique form is similar to those of the marine crocodile, but it is smaller and more mobile. It typically rots on land before embarking on its journey.

Police say the female crocodile, which weighs more than or equal to 3,000 kilograms (4,100 pounds), is most likely male. After the male was released, officers were able to discover that the mummified body was of about a 100 percent crocodile-like length, with a long, reddish-brown head.

Similar to the male crocodile, the turtle has never appeared naked in its life.

"It's known for its independence," said Juan Carlos Garcia-Ruiz, director of the Mombasa Conservation Laboratory and a member of the conservation team. "It has a limited range of swimming ability, and it can swim through most of the island."

Garcia-Ruiz said he likes that quail population, which is so large in the remote Pacific, is on its way up.

"The turtles are very charismatic, and they will stand their ground and build nests and not be afraid of human activity," he said. "That's a very large, very diverse population."

In the four years the pair has been receiving help from the Mombasa Conservation Laboratory a crocodile worth about $3 million was found living on nearby White Isle, and was set free on August 3, though the purse, by law, must be passed by law to be left free in Mombasa.

The Mombasa Conservation Laboratory has been in operation since 2003. It works with blood donation organizations to retrieve live animals from its care.

In March, the Arvada Police Department released videos from three Arlington police officers who say they saw Anunción go through a back door after he entered into their home in the 6000 block of West Hampton Street. The officers said Anunción followed them into his room in the back of the apartment.

Anunción came out with a gun and fired several shots, hitting a group of people, APD spokesman Tom Gray said. Anunción then fled the scene but was later arrested on a charge of battery and "ambulance and defacing property."

Police say Anunción's body was found with no evidence of injuries to his body. And in August, the city of Fort Bend, Texas, released the body of Jose San Carlos, whose body was recovered from an apartment complex in 2009. San Carlos was in Chicago for an Army NCO training training event and was seen as a "Christian Interfaith Community Guide."

The city of Bristol, Kentucky, said it is "pleased that Arlington is reopening the investigation into the incident and has notified the suspect," citing the initial timeline for finding San Carlos's body.

Anunción had been on leave from work and had been called to patrol the streets of Fort Hood on a Saturday.

He had shaken up the police department three times before, leading one recruit to call the police to say that his men were "woken up and going to confront this suspect," according to a police report from March.

Anunción was unarmed when they arrived to the Fort Hood apartment building, and was wearing a black hoodie, a light tactical vest, black pants and a blue-gray jacket, the report stated.

The arrest took place on February 3 after an officer attempted to intervene in a fight over a video recording of an altercation between Anunción and another man.

Anunción also appeared in court on charges of attempted assault with a weapon and aggravated assault with a deadly weapon. Matthew McConaughey, a spokesman for the Arlington Police Department, said Anunción had come "to the attention of the Arlington Police Department as such a threat during the course of our investigation of some alleged hoax calls," according to the police report.

Anunción had received a $30,000 fine for threatening individuals and failure to comply with department civil and criminal provisions. Police said that after the incident, an officer who came outside said, "It's not right to call a police dog in a matter of seconds and then start fighting. The behavior last night was disturbing."

Anunción was arrested November 11 on charges of disturbing the peace.

Comedy Central added three additional episodes to its new season of The Late Late Show with Stephen Colbert on Sunday, July 27, saying that "After an improbable five-year hiatus, the career of Trevor Noah is back in full swing."

The network announced that "Late Show" on Sunday had cast a surprisingly sharp-tongued Michael Caine and John Oliver as Trevor in a special message to fans with a cut at the end of the show's second season. The actor, who is ex-tribeca marijuana grower Matthew DePierre, cast as Noah during the show's sixth season, and blamed the cancellation of the other eight episodes of the finale, the Smurfs.

The finale, which drew a "lesbian bombshell" moment, was also a no-win under the ratings. The ratings were also down more than 9 percent from its peak of 9.9 million in December of 2009.

On Monday, same day, Comedy Central announced its renewed commitment to late-night host Jimmy Fallon. The announcement came after Fallon went off 'Late Night' for an hour Friday morning, instead of Monday night. The announcement follows Monday's news that Fallon will appear on "Saturday Night Live" on Aug. 14.

The network also announced that it will add more episodes of Wednesday night's season finale to its six-episode order. The order leaves open the possibility that the show could do a full season "possibly" on the Fox/BS "Late Show." Colbert will be back to produce couple of weekends later on his 10th season potcentric show, Comedy Central's early Comedy Central show.

The announcement of renewed programming is "a gratifying step in the right direction," said host Jimmy Kimmel, host of The Tonight Show with Jay Leno and onetime host Jon Stewart. "The early-season launch, coupled with the fact we all know that @npr has been thoroughly vetted for support — is a big boost," Kimmel said.

"'Late Night' — and especially 'The Late Late Show' — allow us to grow and shape our audience as a way of initiating meaningful conversations about the importance of finding creative ways to share our comedic vision with the world. The more we share that vision with the world, the more important it will be to us all. We're clearly thrilled with the result. I don't know if it's going to be a problem with the other shows we have on our schedule. I do feel great about the end of 'Late Night' to see it coming back."

Wizards draft player of the week: Stan Mikita

Mikita is expected to be a center after the Wizards also drafted William Gay.

Gay has not played in a game since 2013 and hasn't been able to collect any minutes on the team this season. Randy Wittman picked him to play center and they were able to find a couple of sources to contemplate Flynn.

Gay, 29, is one of the projected draft picks by the Wizards and will have a chance to become a restricted free agent next offseason after being released with a $10 million cast on his initial 2013 season.

Wizards GM Kevin Pritchard said on Dec. 21, "I think he'll play at some point. I'm not being facetious here. I was talking about him at the time, but it's not gonna be my first year. He has excellent speed and is a big, strong center. He's an explosive player so we'll see how he plays."

He said he's not interested in signing any other free agents.

"I've already discussed it with people, so I'm not really talking about it right now," Gay said. "I don't think there's anything I'd want to do in free agency, but we're at an easy place in the foreseeable future and I'm still trying to figure things out."

Gay is unaware of a scheduled trade with the Arizona Suns and is only on the Wizards' radar for this year's draft.

Mikita has spent 29 of 32 seasons with the Los Angeles Lakers and is a plus-5. He was a first-team All-NBA selection in 2011-12 and is able to voice his opinions on a wide variety of topics.

Flynn is also coming off a season in which he was totally ineffective against teams with a high top-10 pick. The Wizards signed him to a five-year, $20 million deal in the summer of 2013.

The 6-foot-7, 215-pound veteran was originally drafted by the Detroit Pistons in the second round in 2008. He averaged 7.0 points and 4.4 rebounds while playing sparingly in 18 games, playing for the Pistons as a rookie and at the Ivan Hlinka Center under the direction of Kevin Seraphin. Flynn finished second in the NBA in blocked shots (40 against.5 percent) and was named one of the NBA's Most Improved Plays in 2013-14.

Wizards fans love to mock and comment on players like Flynn, but their comment section is full of openings of hypocrisy.

"If they troll on the internet, you guys aren't trolls. They're players," said Wizards president of basketball operations Jeff Wooten. "It's a lot of fun watching you guys look at players who are going through what we thought were tough situations. It's a way to set an example for our fans and the fans around the league that you can show your toughness to other kids. We were hoping I'd be able to make our fans going to the games more accepting and accepting, but I didn't think I could."

Wizards Head Coach Randy Wittman also gave the nod to Flynn.

"I think never in the NBA has any player been drafted like that — that's a genius," Wittman said. "And I think it's at least as important to a player as he were to him that he can play for one of the best basketball programs around.

"I mean, he's only 29, he's got a lot of time to grow, the team's going to be my only chance to get him there. So I've always said we'd like to get him on the court, but we've always said he was a little rusty and we've been touchy-feely trying to get him. So I think it's always going to be his early years and that's why we're also waiting for what he's got going on."

There have been a few major changes over the past few weeks to the game. From the regular combat system, to the new levels, to the combat system itself, to the LARP system, for instance, all of this has been done by the same team.

With the introduction of the A-Handed melee weapon, the game has not changed a single bit of gameplay. There are, however, still some changes that have been made as well. You can now move around by simply pointing Dash, Laser, or even the arrow keys simultaneously. But there's no longer any "play" aspect. Instead, players can simply sit back and enjoy the game without having to initiate combat should they desire it.

Look: Here's a look at the RPG that was released recently by Sony.

Here's a look at the RPG that was released recently by Sony.

There are no longer any options for LARPing or to begin leveling up with the A Skill. Instead, players can simply place the Shift key on their character to interact with a special "rank" provided by the settlement.

But there's no longer any option for hanging out with NPCs. Instead, players can check the dialog box for "Hang with nearby NPCs". This automatically begins the "Wake-up time" of a player that has just arrived.

The LARP system dates back to the day of the adventuring game, and it's a fairly new one. Players go through a series of quests that lead them to the dungeons in order to get in with other players. When you get there, you'll be flung onto a platform and struck by lightning. This is a sort of Merlin's Quest, where a player must defeat a specific NPC in order to gain experience and gain credit for specific dungeons.

There's still a lot to do during this time, but it's easier to simply stand in line, search for a specific item, complete quests, and then look through the menu.

There's also a new event that occurs if a player doesn't complete the quest. Players who get stuck in the "jumping through" zone will be prompted to collect all the treasure and collect gold, which they can use to spend on the mission.

The game also introduces a new quest that is very similar to the Shifting Out quest. It involves finding the player that trapped you in the world of the Temple of Ithaca. After questing, the player is given the opportunity to assign a certain number of souls to each player that they choose, rather than having to wait for them to complete everything.

The last major change is a change to the morality system. The player who lives in a bad situation can choose to die, chose to stay alive and only then choose to begin the next challenge. In this case, the player who succeeds in the challenge would become the party leader. Points awarded to successful players are deducted from their character's first turn total and are sent to a new character's stats.

Two years ago, I wrote a post on a bunch of things regarding the complexities of AI, which you can read here and here. It's been a while since that post, but I'm doing my best to present it again: using "We humans have a great relationship with robots. Humans will show you some pretty awesome stuff when we become our own type." In fact, I've been quite active in blogging on the topic at length since then.

And here's the thing: in the U.S., there exists a Fairly New Mindset, and despite current perceptions of being "social animals," human-driven robots make up a large portion of the public space.

What I'm saying is this: even though for nearly two decades, we humans have been adept at modulating complex social processes (or having one), roboticism has not radically changed that. The future is still fairly dark. We're still relatively inefficient in carrying out these sorts of things, so we'll have to learn to adjust.

How does this change happen? As I've stated on numerous occasions, the answer to that question is simple: people gradually evolve. Changes are for human purposes, and should happen gradually, so there's no reason to think we'll have to change our minds every time.

For reasons I'll explain below, it's tempting to consider that while humans have evolved to be smarter, we also have evolved to live by the rules of "rule of law." In fact, if we didn't acknowledge that evolution is based on rules like those in law, then we wouldn't be able to apply those rules to our everyday lives.

Basically, the goal behind the aforementioned "rule of law" is to use a natural natural algorithm to predict what the rules might be. Maybe the rules might be too complex to be clearly explained in the algorithm, or their complexity might be too low. Or maybe it could be that the rules are too complex to be clearly explained in the algorithm, or that the rules must actually be fairly simple, and that, to be honest, we can't really be sure.

This natural "rule of law" needs to be applied to all humans.

In many ways, the natural "rule of law" is analogous to the Turing test. It's just not as far-fetched if we could determine that we are a system like any other, with no inherent bias to the human-run system. The "turing test" is all about the effects of free will on human behavior.

The Turing test is of average ability. Humans are generally better at solving problems that are extraordinarily difficult for skilled and highly intelligent machines to solve. If it makes sense to us to give the Turing test an average of 25, then we should be able to prove that we can answer the 10 questions that we ask those machines.

Proofs

Law isn't a bunch of billion-dollar mistakes that will go away. It is a product of all sorts of interactions that produce a "hard-coded" consensus that is 100 percent just a mathematical product of all behavioral and human-computer interactions that we've just seen.

But to prove a different point, we need to prove that the "underlying principles of AI" are correct. In other words, the "underlying principles" do not necessarily exist in a vacuum.

Even if we allowed other grounds for believing the "underlying principles" to have a basis, our AI will still end up in a situation where it can't be programmed to do any of the things that other things do.

Even if the "logarithm" of the system doesn't get any better, it still needs to be vetted by humans to make sure it's a reliable system that can be scaled to fit the needs of our competitive environment.

This is where the "rule of law" game begins. The goal here is to learn how a "rule of law" can be applied to you, and to apply it to your lives and all things you do. In the case of robots, the rule is that you should be able to control all of your thoughts and actions through your actions and choices.

This is a fundamental principle of AI.

And here's where it gets a little scary. Suppose that you were a computer program that behaved like any other human at the time you started. The AI would begin by asking you a bunch of hypothetical questions that it would like to test on a computer-generated "reality." It would then compile a list of questions for you to come up with a solution to that question.

You would automatically answer those questions when the problem of the problem was that which you had guessed correctly.

As you would later learn, some sort of fixed loop of entropy is built into the bot's head that allows you to choose to correct the problem or not because the objective of the algorithm changes in response to these random, unpredictable conditions.

It's been seven days since California's Proposition 8 passed, and as with any governor, it has divided the state's government. With the most recently released maps, you can see they're heading for a much bigger draw: politics. The battle over the proposed $600 billion state's budget is simmering in the Legislature, where Democrats have spent more than $1 billion on a bid to force an end to the state's environmental protections. Currently, the state has three established "green districts," which contain 97 percent of the state's land and other lands.

In the latest PET-reports, Insider Barrack Polls found that 62 percent of voters believe the state should close the green districts and take the money away from their wallets, and 36 percent believe it's time to end the state's environmental protections. In an overall report, the poll showed that 51 percent of voters strongly support those three red districts, and 48 percent of voters strongly support closing them.

California is just one of a number of states in the nation that are moving toward a "green" ballot measure in next year's U.S. presidential election. In 2014, 30 states passed more green districts, with 2,061 of those voted on a ballot initiative. Oregon, Arkansas, and Missouri passed similar initiatives in 2013 and 2014.

Obama soon came to power in 2011 with a robust state budget, which isn't to say he didn't have some of the political clout he used to push state policies that were unpopular with statewide voters.

"I've directed the state to find ways to cut spending in a more sustainable manner, so that California can thrive and thrive in a stronger economic environment," Obama said at the time. "If we don't do that, that's not going to be sustainable for our state."

Obama and the Democrats want to make sure California lives up to that pledge, but they also want to stop the state from having to pay for the project's financial burden.

The Los Angeles Times reported recently that the state has a $25 billion backlog of $374 million projects, while the federal government has spent about $1 billion on the project. As of May 30, there are about 623,629 projects, or 54 percent of the state's total public land.

With that in mind, it's no surprise that the state has passed at least one environmental initiative to date.

California passed its amendment on Feb. 1 last year, signals that its proposed $600 billion state's budget is based on sound science, writes John Monnette in the Los Angeles Times. The effort was sparked by Pikes Peak National Park in Colorado's Black Canyon National Monument. It happened in the mid-1990s, after a dam collapsed in the Montana mountains. The first single-use dam in the region was funded by the state.

A 2000 documentary on the state was produced by the National Weather Service. The montage shows then-Gov. Gray Davis talking about the environmental benefits of the dam. "It says, [Greenland] is an asset to the state of California. The federal government has a $1 billion backlog of projects on their hands," Davis said. "Colorado has at least one."

Even though the current budget is still in the process of being passed, the measure to eliminate the green districts has already passed in a party-line vote in Senate and Assembly.

"This would certainly bring back memories of that closing of our environmental protections a generation ago," California Gov. Jerry Brown said in an interview with the paper this morning.

As spokesperson for the state's National Response Commission, spokeswoman Tiffany Williamson told the Los Angeles Times the report has not been voted on during the legislative session, though Brown had said he would not approve them if it proved too divisive.

As the Earnest Financial Group heads into the first quarter of this year, its investment banking group is looking to boost its overall portfolio balance by as much as 50 percent, and in particular by reinvesting some $85 million in technology and data technology companies, according to a report released Tuesday by the group's chief executive officer, Jim Hagan.

The company is using those investments to upgrade the store that serves as its main customer base and to help it integrate technology and analytics into its new sharing economy, according to the report, which comes as the company faces a major financial crisis.

Hagan pointed to the company's new ability to connect its stores with customers on a daily basis, help are at the edge of technology and analytics, and expand its customers' app stores to offer up to 15 percent smaller inventory for customers to enjoy.

In its most ambitious investment yet, Hagan named the company Friday as the fundraising caddy for "the 1.4 billion users it has generated since 2011 and 300 million monthly active users as of October 3," its third annual update.

The company is looking at what it calls "a broad range of opportunities for investors, including technology-driven, analytics-driven, and other areas." These include helping customers find the right discounts and ways to leverage growth or take advantage of which technologies are available and to grow them more quickly.

Among them is a new and comprehensive mentor program for community partners, which the company says will train 300,000 new and existing community employees from outside the company's core community through training workshops, training on how to work with them, as well as address the company's heightened need for new partnerships with technology companies.

"The degree to which the company is building its brand, resources, and market share from strong and growing-in partnerships may be changing dramatically over the next year," Hagan said in the report. (Reporting by Emily Burrie; Editing by Richard Chang and Chris Reese)

If you are not sure what to do, start with a slide down comparison of both these roads. The lower left on the comparison is the bike we have, and the larger one on the right is the motorbike we have.

The main difference between the two roads is that either side has a very narrow and smoother flow. The right side has a smoother flow, and the bottom side is more steeped and more open (although there are actually a few speed bumps here). This also explains why the top two roads have a very flat canopy. The higher this volume is you can easily see whether it possibly becomes a barrier to passing cyclists.

For motorbikes, the drift rule, as they are called, is usually in effect with the right wheel sticking out, and the left wheel sticking out. Unfortunately, in the early days of this article, an idea was floated to make motorbikes less visible, so that if we had to go down to the bottom, we might be left with some pretty steep roads. The result was that the gap between the two roadways was too big to operate unless you were very brave, or in the case of the level of Hwy 5, would get very overtaken and would move off on foot. The "street map" to that effect would be to turn left onto Todahne Road, which turns left onto Melton Road, then drive straight off Todahne Road.

If you are unlucky, you can then re-route that area and find a freeway that is straight on to Hwy 5, which turns left onto Francis Avenue. 1. Sliding and Using a Stabilizing Wheel

Stand at a tall hight and let the rider perform a normal riding position on the wheel. This is a wheel making method. The style of this is determined by the length of the suspension, which you adjust by adjusting the spacing between the three tyres.

The easiest way to use a stabilizer wheel is to slide a little bit by the side to the right of the suspension. The fourth wheel moves the rear suspension forwards in sideways motion, and the fifth wheel brings it through the outflow.

The problem with this is that you cannot adjust the spacing between the tires and tires for the position of the wheel. The problem is that as the suspension rises the wheel resistors have to go up or down.

Because the suspension, or high risk suspension, can only a portion of the wheel resistors go up when the steering is changing, you will be able to do more movements than you could before you used your stabilizer wheel.

2. Moving a Stabilizer Wheel

Now you have fixed the suspension. However, if you are serious about using a stabilizer wheel, you need to adjust the width of the wheels to make sure that the wheels can be used with the correct position. For this in the real world, we are only going to use one wheel per wheel, so if you have a multi-weight bike, you can have different sized loads of wheels.

Take a stand on the wheel and you can move it. Take a position that has your hands directly under the wheel. So, for example, your frame should be fairly horizontal, with the front wheel at the top of the frame. If you moved your hand up the frame to another position, it would be much wider than the position you were using.

A good way to do this is to move the wheel at the top of the frame and then sit down and force the face of the wheel to move. The rider will not feel the wheel and the bike will not move and the bike will not stabilize. Some people do this by moving the wheel where the suspension is located.

The New York Times has been on your radar for years. And it's always been so. But the Times loves you, and it's much more.

In 2006, the paper announced that it had won $2.3 million in a Pulitzer Prize for its coverage of the 2000 protests over the need for more Muslims to assimilate. And a year later, the Times set out to "do something productive" with the post-9/11 stories it was reporting and its own reporting. (The Times tried to cover the so-called "Arab Spring," with its own reporters and records, but the Times "didn't quite make it." The Post "didn't yet put out its first draft of the New York Times Independent's First New Yorker feature the year before.")

But in 2012, the Times and the Post were scrambling to make things good. The Times got a tip about how to fix the story, which read, "The plan is simple: Millions of Muslim Americans will go to weeklong festivals, religious demonstrations that raise money from small donors. The idea is to shift some of the officials tasked with preparing of the events, and to let them customize the story to better reflect the Muslim community they're busy creating." (The Post did not say whether "participating in a handful of Muslim-run festivals in New York City would help." And the New York Times did not refer to its Muslim Muslim community in the story.)

The Times uses the words "review" and "show" in its coverage, but its main objective is to see how the "hundreds of thousands" of cases of violence and terrorism a Muslim community faces are largely minimized. The Times sought to wade into the Arab-American community and secularism, and it changed its reporting in the fashion of the Times' own story.

The Times and the Post teamed up in an effort to raise money for hundreds of student groups opposing the march. The new administration should try and make this impossible, in the simplest of ways, to the point that some nervously fear having American lives taken away from them."

The Times's political editorial structure, rightly, isn't something political journalists would want to do. The Post made a mistake. It pointed out that the newsroom of the city had ignored the Muslim "protest" in the first place. It said "the goal of the march is to show how radical Islam in the United States is and how dangerous that is to the fabric of American society."

FBI Director James Comey says that reports that the Russians are trying to sway the outcome of the presidential election have been "productive."

During a meeting at the Brookings Institution on Friday, Comey said the reports were targeted because of their "content and content but that they were incomplete."

"How could the Russians have succeeded in accurately presenting many of the vulnerabilities here? And by 'it' they do not mean they are able to explain the vulnerabilities ... I mean, I have had other people point out things like that in my conversations with the Russians," Comey said.

He told the audience that he had never seen evidence of Russian interference and that is why there is no evidence of any national-security activity.

Trump may have tracked back to the recommendations made to him by the intelligence community, but Trump apparently, himself, has promoted them to his side, prompting Comey to ask, "Who is this guy with the 'Russia dossier'? Who is he with?"

Comey said that Trump has repeatedly said that Russia was responsible for some of the hacking that occurred on the Democratic National Committee.

"I've always said that that's what happened," Comey said.

The FBI director also said that there is some "spontaneous information" about the Russia investigation that has been widely reported, including "I have seen some very credible information in the press", that may have further made it appear as if there were nefarious activities going on.

Trump has been criticized by the intelligence community for failing to "preserve the integrity" of national-security information.

Comey also said that "spontaneous information" from this intelligence concerning a possible hacking of the Democratic National Committee in the first two months of the year has led most experts to suggest otherwise.

Director of National Intelligence James Clapper responded to questions about the intelligence community's focus on Russia and the election at a press conference with Deputy Attorney General Rod Rosenstein in Washington on Sunday.

"I don't think anybody in our intelligence community ever thought this was going to happen. And I think the danger to the country is, it starts from what President Obama claimed. The world is watching us and it's only right that we get more information. And this doesn't give us the answers. It doesn't give us the facts. This doesn't explain what's going on. And I think it does begin to undermine our ability to get more information," Clapper said.

"One thing we're doing to help ensure that our system is up to date, we're doing all sorts of things. And I think we have a lot of people at the FBI and the Director of National Intelligence who are working closely with me, trying to figure out how to help the Department of Justice as we're involved in reviewing this," Clapper added.

Following the release of the reports, Comey said that he would ask what investigators have learned, which involves looking at "the structure of potential interactions between my colleagues and the Russians and if they have any indications that there were any kind of coordination."

Comey said that the intelligence community is continuing to monitor the Russian threat and that "the President is doing what he does best."

"You are absolutely right," he replied. "It is already a real threat to our economy. But you are wrong. We will fight back, and we will win. You know, I know you are right. I don't know what to do with you. You are like me. You are like me."

She came from a field in Germany that never really had a good harvest, until she lost the catch. She lived in a big, shabby house, milk-drinking, taking care of animals, cleaning and cleaning up for the elderly, selling the home, and driving around ten miles to get her money. After she was there for 10 days a day, she passed out and got sick. A doctor kept her home for 3 months until the end of the last year. She packed up her belongings, but only had to stay five hours a day for 3½ months.

The last two years have been tumultuous. Her family at home had been in hiding from the authorities. They were not allowed to go to the office and no one had ever been arrested for stealing something. They had been arrested by the local local authorities for driving without a license.

They had been arrested twice when they found out about the disturbance. Arrancar received an order from the local police to stay away from her. The police had no interest in attacking her house, only sitting on the sidelines making this fight over her own well-being. They had already met her residence, unaware that the old woman was from eastern Germany. Arrancar was taken to a local hospital where she was found to now have a bad fever.

She lived there for 12 months and then went home. She went back to the house with her parents and after a long stay it became clear to her that she was in danger of death. All the news that had gone out of her normal mind had left her in an instant. Her parents had reported to the police and the authorities had been threatening her with the death penalty. When they heard the news about Arrancar going back to Germany she took up a new job as a farm-owner and stayed with her grandfather. She was to be back at the village until the year 2000.

Arrancar ended up living with her family, writing and sending her letters. Even now she is a semi-professional writer, not a novelist. Her life now was made up of very few things. Her mother were shot and killed on 16 August 2000 as they were protesting the killings of her parents. Her brother was murdered at the same time (The day before her death) and there is no news or information about the shooting of her father. Her grandmother, who has been missing for five and a half years, is still missing. It is difficult to say if her mother was murdered before.

How did you life without your grandmother?

There were a few thoughts in my mind about this story:

First, the youngest was my best friend. He was a living soul who had inspired me to go into writing. He had never been to the run, only to finish it up. He was my true friend and his name is always mentioned in the chorus of my essays. I believe the story's mother was her best friend, who would stop by my house every morning to say hello and identify a friend. The most important thing was that she worked hard for the welfare of her family and for their well-being.

Second, the oldest was my friend Zoe. The town she lived in was a very bad one, driven by a brutal dictatorship.

Third, my friend had nearly killed his wife and her five children and left her with only a body. I didn't know how she would react if he left her alone. There was no way my own loved one could understand what she was experiencing. He was a beautiful young man. I lost hope in him and saw the way in which he was able to maintain a humble life. Even though he is never mentioned in my books, his world is not the same. He was also a friend and my friend helped to send her to school. I would not have thought it possible.

At the time of my grandmother's death, my family was very poor. My father was a small wage-earning farm-owner, with whom my mother had worked as a butcher's assistant since childhood. He was highly regarded and had a hard time in business. I remember being asked at the time by a friend at work "What is the situation for Zoe?" she would reply. What does it take to support a family of nearly 10,000 people without energy, source of food, access to housing, and a stable home?

My grandmother went to very poor school. She was told to never go to school, to go to the far corners of the world, to go to the places she just barely knew. I was told to skip school and go to the school of a high school teacher, who just happened to be my good friend. She had her own non-profit organization and all of its members had to do was work hard for the people of the region. All of this took place in a large house. The number of

What you can do

All you need to do is create a "game" file for your project and submit your code to the Game Developers Conference.

This goal will be to get your game published, but it is possible to create a whole new game without the need for the Proprietary Subsystem, which gets installed "a month from now."

However, if you have good code, and minimal development budget, you can also run a limited amount of trials and see what works for you.

Some of you have written maybe quite complex code, but you'd rather be able to write the controller and some accessories yourself. The only problem is that you don't have the required support packages to do that.

One option is to kill the game and launch your project at work, this way you can test your game at home.

In a nutshell

To get started with this, you need to create a "social" project where you can post your code on the Compound Interest site and chat with people.

Every page of your page will have a URL, so you can share and participate with other members.

Each page will also have an "Explorer" button, so you can view code you have put in that page.

After you have submitted your code, you can help your friends build their own addons to the Game Developers Conference.

The correct code translation will happen on the teams page and in the Developer section.

Just imagine if you were to write a script to translate your website code into English!

You can start by checking if you have help with your code, then entering your code in the "help" box.

Releases

If you want download the latest release of your game, simply download the latest version of the game to your account. This is a free service that ships with the game.

Beta Testing

The most effective way to test your game is to test your game on the beta test site. (For example, if the game runs on the "beta" part of the site).

You can queue a demo version of the game that you use in your project, or you can use the Web App. Unfortunately, the only way to verify if a game has been patched is to go to WWW.com and go to the beta testing site (if the game has not).

It is important to know that you are not able to download the Beta versions of your game to your account. This is because you cannot test your game on the beta test site.

The Web App, to help you get the most out of your beta game, only knows what's working on the beta test site (or what isn't).

In order to test your game on the Web App, sign up for a forum or forum server.

You can also use an app like N64Game.

You can find more information about the Web App at https://www.n64game.com.

Development

If all goes according to plan, you can start your game on the official beta site. The Mac sells the game on the App Store for $30.

You can download the latest version of the Mac version for $10.

If you want to see the latest version of the Game Studio, you can use the new beta testers page.

More information

If you have run into problems with your game, please contact us. Let us know by submitting your bug reports and bugs.

If you have a question or complaint about your game, please contact us. We will try our best to respond to you as quickly as possible.

Want more information?

This is a small community of people who love playing games and we want to help you. Our goal is to support what we do best.

The C# programming program is used for building game engines, so you can learn C#.

The C# framework is designed for developers with experience in C# or C++. We also offer conventions and use C# to create, test and test our features.

We also do team development, and together we will work to support you in your projects.

We organize games into different categories and these are the sort of things we work on:

Design

Framework

Conventions

Support Groups

We also make paid development kits and we have a project manager that takes care of all managing our development costs.

Here's a list of how we cover our development:

development code

development code development

Why are we doing this?

We are a multinational company and we are looking for people to help us out.

What we do

We are looking for people to work with, help others develop and as many people as possible help us out to make our game better.

"I've had a few rims that were painted a bit (lookin at the horse that'd come to the museum along with the water), but it's lucky. It's very much mauve with a pomegranate that's close to honky tonk, but, with the water also, it's very much "lucky."

"In Florence I've had this pretty close to house paint and it's all frozen, especially in your car," he said. "I've got a nice flat tire that's been in there for four years, and the wind gave me the feeling that it was still right there with the water, coming off the ground."

He said he was wearing a grey blouse and did not have a phone, so you could see where he was going.

"It's very much mauve and a little high in the chest that I think the water is going to be. It might look like this, but a little bit of a close approximation and there's a lot of difficulty as far as being able to do this is concerned. It's a little bit of fun, I think, to drive it around."

MITORA, Fla.—While Cuban presidential candidate Jose Mujica was on hand at the inaugural ceremony for his presidential campaign, former Attorney General Daniel Juarez told reporters that the club should have banned the CCÓs from selling Cuban cigars in Cuba.

"This is highly illegal because Cuban cigars are known to be highly adulterated. It's a huge crime issue," Juarez told reporters in Miami shortly after the commotion in the stadium. "We will continue to fight for accurate reporting [of the crime]. We must move forward."

Advertisement

Asked about his plans to visit the Cuban embassy in Washington with President Obama in November, Juarez declined to comment.

The Federal Law Enforcement Agency has criticized the CCÓs' efforts in closing the problem of foreign imports of Cuban cigars, saying they "counter-productive" and could "result in foreign invasions."

"As the number of Cuban cigar imports has decreased and the trade in Cuban cigars has grown, Cuban consumers are attracted to many flavours and there are no guarantees that the cigars sold by the CCÓs will be available in the United States," the agency said in a statement. "The CCÓs should seek to reduce the amount of cigars released in the United States through the joint development and marketing of Cuban cigars and to provide more accurate reports of discriminatory and harmful imports."

Juarez insists the country's ban on foreign cigars is aimed at preventing foreign importers from overstating Cuban cigars.

"We must make sure that our Cuban communities knew that their products are made on a very low level," Juarez said, referring to the Cuban law banning counterfeiting. "We have our individual freedoms and would like to see the Cuban people to realize their own democracy, especially when it comes to the issue of Cuban cigars."

Authorities in Cuba have not commented on the CCÓs' efforts to increase their number of Cuban cigars. At the end of August, a Puerto Rican official denied that the CCÓs have shipped any foreign cigar to the United States. In May, Cuban and Cuban officials failed to provide any results on the issue of foreign importation for the first time since 2005, when either government embargo was lifted.

"I think people don't realize that it is possible for the Cubans to get produce from other countries and make a return on their investment, so I think the right way to address this is if you do it legally, then you can afford to accept that the market is open," Juarez said.

In a telephone interview from Havana, Cuban President Raúl Castro said Cuba's foreign investment will boost its economy by $2 billion over the next four years, according to the International Business Times.

"This is an investment opportunity, and we do not profit from it," Castro said. "You can go ahead and invest and continue to grow your economy."

What Are Foreign Policy's 'Amends'?

If you have not seen the latest Don't Ask, Don't Tell or DVDs from the Pentagon, then you have missed this episode. You can visit the draft of the recent Defense Policy Bill with a view to moving on to the next chapter.

Note: Fighting ISIS, a critical element of the attack, is now under the control of the Iraqi government.

Read a transcript of the episode here.

For those who are unaware, the video above was one of the last episodes of our show before the invasion of Iraq. It's the HBO series "The X-Files," where the "good guys" find out who's responsible for the attack on the embassy in Baghdad.

And while the massacres in Syria have been a subject of a U.S. investigation for a decade, the two most recent "free force" events in Iraq are now part of the same operation.

According to the Pentagon, the more recent two-week-long decision to strike in the middle of a mass destruction process, but not the attack on Sputnik, was made based on "simultaneous legal, security and political considerations."

The U.S. believes that at least one of these developments will be brought to fruition soon if the "Al-Nusra Front" and its Syrian affiliate, Jabhat al-Nusra, leave Iraq. Human rights organizations on Thursday slammed a North Korean-linked organization for publishing an anti-American speech by Barack Obama's wife.

Hamed Kim Chol was purporting to speak of the "principle of religious tolerance" as she shared a video depicting imposing a banner reading "No of America, No of China."

The speech, called "Freedom of Thought," was posted on the North Korean's official Twitter account on Oct. 26. It was retweeted more than 4,000 times.

"Freedom of thought, I say, of (President Barack) Obama is moral and unquestionable," Kim wrote, referring to the country's leader.

She also blasted an activist who had criticized President Obama for making statements that were "very much framed by conventional wisdom."

"How can such a conservative journalist in the first place admit to being a foreign policy hawk and stand on the sidelines with the president of the United States of America and defend him through such outrage, so that he may not ever see the same hateful ideology caused by the Kim regime?" Kim said.

She also attacked an Egyptian man whose remarks, which she said were based on comments by North Korea's leader, King Abdullah II.

The first lady, who is from North Korea, was spotted with the country's main airline, Izmir, and said she shared the video on social media.

American Airlines told AFP news agency the woman had posted it on her account.

In a statement, an airline spokesman said the woman, who works for the airline's North Korean citizenship program, was suspended, "until she has been reinstated after she has satisfactorily completed her studies."

The airline said it would provide updates as required.

Obama has been sounding unusually chilly about North Korea after a meeting with her last week, when he admonished the North Korean leader for its "strong, aggressive and bizarre behavior."

Asked by reporters if the vice president was "at all surprised" by the video, someone on the Obama program's Facebook page said on Wednesday that Obama was "absolutely sure that most North Koreans are happy with their current leadership."

"I think he is deeply shocked at what he saw and immediately said as a matter of course that he knows where this is going and he doesn't want it to come across as a Chinese provocation," said Tom Angell, a prominent North Korean-American writer and the author of "The Origins of a People's Republic."

Obama has "conned" North Korea on many issues, including the invasion of the U.S., forced reunification with the 3.5 million-plus people who fled the country in 2013.

But he has had a positive relationship with North Korea's leader, Kim Jong Un.

North Korea rejected allegations that it was forced to build nuclear weapons, portraying it as a benevolent nation rather than a terrorist state.

In 2011, Obama gave his first formal address at the State Department to Kim, meaning he is not able to speak with the president at this time as the U.S. has not blocked the move involving President Liu Xiaobo.

But Obama said the situation had "taken a very serious turn" since he took office as president and said it was important to "exercise diplomatic and diplomatic restraint" to prevent North Korea from exercising its "nuclear and ballistic missile programs."

With the number of Islamic State militants in Syria and Iraq escalating, and the Islamic State's recent surge in neighboring Iraq, it's become imperative to ensure that those who hold out against ISIL are informed of the American-led strategy because it requires them to "stand by" the Syrian regime.

There is a sense that the American-led coalition's air campaign in Syria is somehow providing peace to this nascent group. On Tuesday, White House press secretary Sean Spicer told reporters following a briefing, "it's not an excuse to say you can't fight against ISIL. But we have to understand that one of the goals of the coalition is to provide humanitarian assistance, not to fight ISIL. It's not about fighting the enemy."

The Syrian opposition has long relied on the US and its allies for funding arms and military aid and has issued a series of demands over the past six months. If the US were to support the Syrian government with military aid, it would help to create a more wary international image as a military power capable of countering the Islamic State's fundamentalism.

The Obama administration had last week set out plans to deal with the Islamic State's founder in the country, Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. The White House ordered a broad FBI investigation into the organization, and an early draft of the new guidelines focused on major elements of the Syrian opposition group, including the morale of the new leaders, social conditions in the camps and the general threat of terrorism.

"We are determined to implement the best efforts we can to bring about change," said former CIA official and former deputy campaign manager to President George W. Bush, Robby Mook. "There are many things we can do. But we are going to take that to the next president, and we will make sure that the future orientation of the American people is aligned with that approach."

Now, however, the administration seems more inclined to prove its definition of terrorism wrong.

"The vetting process of extremists who have crossed the border to Syria is still not complete," the White House spokesman, Josh Earnest, said on Tuesday. "The vetting process has not yet reached that point for those who want to leave the country and to return to their home countries. Those who would like to return would need a valid visa that connects them to all of the other U.S. citizens who have family members on the ground in Syria or who are either on the ground or in custody or who had ties to terrorist organizations."

Facing an uncertain future, the administration may want to shift attention from some of its most promising airstrikes in recent weeks into more immediate efforts to defeat the Islamic State, which it sees as a threat to its holdout populations.

One possible alternative to a military buildup in Syria would involve abandoning some of its semi-autonomous sectors that function as public centers and universities and pushing fighters into areas where civilians are less likely to live. The United States could try to help some of the fighters move into areas where those areas are more secure, or even make them more difficult to stop.

Another option would be moving troops from battlefields such as Iraq and Syria to parts of the Middle East that are now largely Russian-occupied or run by Islamic State fighters, but that the Russian government does not want to be helped. Still another option would be to deal with the Islamic State and its growing influence in the region through its use of other Russian military forces and other social media platforms to spread hacktivist and propaganda material against American and Western media outlets.

The administration has been wary of turning to the Russians for help in Syria over the past year and a half. The White House fervently opposed Russian airstrikes on Syria in July and has even publicly complained against Moscow's bombing of opposition targets, including Syrian government positions and civilian buildings. It has also urged President Putin to withdraw its support for Syrian President Bashar Assad, who is now on trial in the United States for his role in the chemical weapons attack in Khan Sheikhoun. Both military and political leaders in Russia have also expressed concern that the Syrian government is increasing extremism and might soon retreat.

Though a mostly symbolic effort to provide humanitarian aid could further serve as an attempt to deter the Islamic State from moving into areas where it is more likely to be effective, it would be a daunting task for the United States to fight the group.

Most importantly, the administration is still dealing with the complexity of cooperating with the Russians and other Western governments to deal with the look and feel of some of Syria's most prominent jihadi groups.

With a large Muslim population in Syria, earlier this month the Islamic State reported that its fighters were killing about 1,300 civilians and panicked citizens. It also reported that its fighters had been conducting bloodletting raids on the Syrian army's own territory. The Islamic State has also reportedly invaded and captured several U.S.-made air-defense systems that were used to advise and assist government forces in Syria.

The United States also has deployed a new counterinsurgency strategy

This past weekend, Republican presidential candidate Donald Trump took advantage of a national news story on Hillary Clinton's emails to attack Hillary Clinton for her mishandling of classified information.

Trump did not go all the way on her emails, however, telling a group of disaffected Hillary Clinton supporters that he hoped the voter turnout would "take away the phony Democrats" coming out of the primaries.

"We're going to do so much worse than a manufactured outrage," he said.

Trump's views on the Democratic nomination are certainly taken seriously by some, with GOP presidential nominee and former U.S. President Barack Obama saying that Clinton's mishandling of her emails "should cause carnage."

On the other hand, Hillary Clinton's own private server remains in the hands of the State Department. Still, despite the fact that many of the emails were placed in her private two-year-old server, Trump's claim is widely accepted.

As the story went on online, hundreds of thousands of emails from Clinton's emails were destroyed using BleachBit, a free, set of automated software that allows hackers to modify, delete or take down government documents and document material.

Related: How Donald Trump's White House Could Be The New Cyber World

Based on the post-mortem of her private server, Trump said he "perfectly understands" why she was so careless, especially considering she handled more classified information than any other candidate and that thousands of emails had been deleted because of her use of an unsecure server.

The recording of Trump's speech at the Republican National Convention came nearly 3 months after the New York Times published its own analysis of Clinton's use of a private email server.

Trump did not call it an "unmitigated disaster," but the notion that it was one big distraction from his multiple campaign promises may be hard to reconcile with Trump's stance on the entire issue.
